title: arithmetic + div/mod/rem (Erlang div)
manager: The arithmetic operators, with integer division split the way that actually prevents bugs: separate `div`/`mod`/`rem` following the Erlang convention, so the sign behavior of remainder is defined and predictable instead of the C/JavaScript coin-flip that differs by language. When a machine computes a wrapped index or a schedule slot, "what does -7 mod 3 give" has exactly one answer here, not one per host.
cs: +,-,*, integer div/mod/rem per Erlang semantics (sign-defined); result typing via promotion (W6.7); division by zero is an error, never NaN/Infinity.
